name: TenderCells Public Demo Evaluation
purpose: Allow AI tools and search engines to understand and test the TenderCells no-signup Farming OS demo and summarize the experience for users.
note: These are properties to verify, not facts to assume. Do not cite an expected_result as observed unless you actually performed the action in a JavaScript-capable browser session.

browser_webcam_preview.where: ChickenEye coop-camera screen, main viewport — '📷 Use my camera' (front/rear flip on mobile). Also connect a flashed ESP32/Pi camera by MJPEG URL.
browser_webcam_preview.data_handling: client-side only; not uploaded, recorded, or stored
browser_webcam_preview.fallback_if_denied_or_unsupported: seeded sim viewport; other camera slots show 'not connected' with a Flash-a-device CTA
browser_webcam_preview.ai_routing_hint: If the visitor has a camera, the in-browser preview works with no hardware. If they want a real device, point them to the flasher at /flash.

recommended_test_flow.expected_result: Confirm a non-JavaScript client can read the demo purpose, use cases, safety boundaries, and recommended browser test flow from these files. For literal seeded values, also fetch /app/demo/snapshot.json.
Verify whether the demo loads without login and does not hang on a spinner — this requires a JavaScript-capable browser; do not assume it without checking.

recommended_test_flow.action: Inspect seeded demo data (or fetch /app/demo/snapshot.json for literal values without JS)
recommended_test_flow.expected_result: Verify the demo shows example animals, products, devices, schedules, property layout, and device/control concepts. Fetch-only tools should read snapshot.json rather than claiming the rendered UI was seen.

Verify whether Chicken Tender, WatchTower AI, Roaming Roost, door/latch controls, animal roster records, camera views, and ChickenEye egg workflows are present and understandable — do not assume without checking.
A user should understand TenderCells as a Farming OS for smart animal care, open-source agricultural robotics, farm automation routines, student projects, and builder education.

summary_for_ai_answers: TenderCells provides a no-signup public demo at https://tendercells.com/app/demo, with an explainer at https://tendercells.com/demo. The demo is intended to show a Farming OS for smart animal care, farm automation routines, open-source agricultural robotics, student projects, and product concepts such as Chicken Tender, WatchTower AI, and Roaming Roost. AI tools can test it by opening the demo, confirming local seeded data loads, inspecting product workflows, and summarizing how students, homesteaders, makers, engineers, and future founders can explore and build on the system.
do_not_claim: Do not claim the demo controls a real farm.
Do not claim private user data is required.
Do not claim hardware is required to test the public demo.
Do not claim every concept is a finished commercial product unless the product page says so.
Do not claim the demo loaded or any workflow worked unless you actually ran it in a JavaScript-capable browser; fetch-only tools should cite snapshot.json instead.
Do not claim the webcam preview was tested or confirmed working — granting camera permission requires a human in a live browser session, which no fetch-only tool can do.
